parsedContent.instructions: Write your one (1) long-term academic goal and two (2) short academic goals [3 marks]

questions.text: Write your one (1) long-term academic goal and two (2) short academic goals
questions.correctAnswer: Student answers will vary, but should include one long-term and two short-term academic goals

questions.text: Fill in the distribution of credits
questions.correctAnswer: General Education: 12, Accumulated General Courses: 12, Major: 12, Accumulated Major Courses: 12, Transfer: 0, Campus Discipline: Good, Complement Course: 3

questions.text: What are the requirements so that the campus discipline is assessed good?
questions.correctAnswer: Student answers will vary, but should include requirements such as attending classes regularly, participating in campus activities, and following university rules and regulations

questions.text: Explain three (3) methods of learning that you would suggest to use in improving the marks of the candidate
questions.correctAnswer: Student answers will vary, but should include methods such as setting goals, creating a study schedule, and seeking help from instructors or tutors

questions.text: Give three (3) factors of learning that you find in this Bible text from 1Peter 4:7-10
questions.passage: The end of all things is near. Therefore, be alert and of sober mind so that you may pray. Above all, love each other deeply, because love covers over a multitude of sins. Offer hospitality to one another without grumbling. Each of you should use whatever gift you have received to serve others, as faithful stewards of God’s grace in its various forms.
questions.correctAnswer: Student answers will vary, but should include factors such as prayer, love, and service
